items.title: Murray & Co and its Sunday auction: the famous ‘hammer’ of Madras nears the century mark
items.summary: Since February 10, 1927, the hammer at the auction house Murray & Co has not had a break except for two seasons: for a few weeks during a relocation to a new address and then during the pandemic. The operational space might have changed a few times, but its place in people’s mind space has not

items.title: S. Janaki: The Isai Arasi who established the voice of the rural, female heroine
items.summary: Janaki amma’s voice gave contour to the heroines, their fears, their desires, their hopes and their dreams, even as Bharathiraja wrote them lion’s space on the screen

items.title: Poor monsoon and no Cauvery water: distress in Tamil Nadu’s delta region
items.summary: All is not well in the famed ‘Granary of South India’. Between a poor southwest  monsoon and lack of water in the Cauvery, crops and farmers are left high and dry. The kuruvai cultivation area in Thanjavur alone has dipped to around 89,000 acres this season from around 1,94,000 acres in the previous cultivation year.  This report describes the situation on the ground

items.title: 99 years old,  Gem & Co and Kalathi Shop in Chennai show an enterprise need not have muscle to survive time
items.summary: As these retail outlets march towards the centenary mark, an attempt to decipher that which makes them click: Gem & Co at NSC Bose Road in George Town has persisted with its belief that fountain pens, even artisanal ones, have a place in this digital age. And Kalathi Shop, operating from a hole in the wall at North Mada Street in Mylapore, is essentially a petty shop that has people beating a path to its door for its rose milk

items.title: Judge a purchase by its cover: how three sustainable outlets in Chennai make sure packaging does not leave carbon footprints
items.summary: Restore at Kottivakkam on East Coast Road offers a 2 per cent discount on purchases if the customer brings their own bag; Kazhani Native Farms in Kottivakkam has placed a cardboard box at the entrance inviting customers to drop off extra shopping bags for others to reuse; and Tula India in Adyar encourages customers to bring their own bags, and those who forget are handed handmade newspaper bags
